2017Neutral (â0.4)Positive +0.7+5% AboveGood rains MH/GJ+6% goodâ Steady
2018Neutral (â0.1)Negative â0.6â10% BelowModerate deficitâ8% dropâ Price rise
2019El NiĂąo (+0.5)Positive +1.4+9% ExcessHeavy MH/TG+11% bestâ Bearish
2020La NiĂąa (â1.3)Positive +0.8+9% ExcessSurplus all states+9% strongâ COVID crash
2021La NiĂąa (â1.0)Neutral +0.1+0.4% NormalNear normal+4% goodâ Sharp rally

INDIA
India 2025-26 crop revised to 288.7 lakh bales â down 6.5% YoY. Maharashtra and Gujarat see largest production declines.
Mar 2026 Âˇ CAI
INDIA
Cotton imports surge to 49.1 lakh bales in 2025-26, up 18.7% YoY as domestic crop shortfall drives mill buying from Australia, US and Brazil.
Mar 2026 Âˇ CAI
INDIA
Guj-29 domestic rates at âš54,271/Candy. Prices under pressure from higher imports and sluggish domestic demand from spinning mills.

ProcessingâŚ
đ Excel Format â 2 Required Sheets
Sheet: Crop_Calendar
Col A: State name
Cols B+: Phase pairs (Start | End)
Values: month index 0â9
0.5 = mid-month
Leave blank = not applicable
Sheet: State_Info
Col A: State name
Col C: Area Âˇ Col D: Variety
Col E: Season Âˇ Col F: Irrigation
Col G: Sow peak Âˇ Col H: Harv peak
Col I: Notes / districts
0=May Âˇ 1=Jun Âˇ 2=Jul Âˇ 3=Aug Âˇ 4=Sep Âˇ 5=Oct Âˇ 6=Nov Âˇ 7=Dec Âˇ 8=Jan Âˇ 9=Feb

ProcessingâŚ
đ Expected Excel Format
One sheet per crop â sheet names must match exactly:
Cotton Âˇ Maize Âˇ Groundnut Âˇ Tur Âˇ Soybean Âˇ Paddy

Row 1: Title row (ignored)
Row 2: Crop name in Col A, then year labels (2012-13, 2013-14 âŚ) in Cols B onwards
Rows 3â11: Cost components (Land Prep, Sowing, Fertilizer, Irrigation, etc.)
Row 12: Total cost
Row 13: Yield per acre
Row 14: Price per quintal
Row 15: Gross return
Row 16: Net return
Row 17: ROI (decimal â e.g. 0.57 = 57%)
Unit: âš per Acre for costs Âˇ Qtl/Acre for yield Âˇ âš/Qtl for price
